persona.name: DevOps Dana
persona.summary: Dana is a Senior DevOps Engineer at a fintech company (50-200 employees) who lives in the terminal and CI/CD pipelines. She integrates every tool via API-first workflows and expects webhooks, GitHub integrations, and Terraform support as table stakes. She's a power user who will advocate for a product internally if the developer experience is excellent, but will churn immediately if the API has rough edges.

location_signals: fintech hub (NYC, London, SF inferred from company profile)

goals: Automate team onboarding and project scaffolding via API so engineers don't touch the UI
Set up event-driven webhook pipelines to sync project updates with internal CI/CD and Slack
Integrate GitHub PRs and commits directly into project tracking without manual status updates
Build custom dashboards to give engineering leads real-time visibility into sprint health
pains: GraphQL endpoint has rough edges — schema inconsistencies slow down automation scripting
Manual configuration required for each new project instead of infrastructure-as-code support
Team invite workflow requires UI interaction rather than API-driven provisioning
Webhook reliability is unclear — no delivery guarantees or retry visibility in the dashboard
motivations: Reduce toil: every manual step in a workflow is a tax on engineering velocity
Gain peer respect by shipping toolchain improvements that the whole team benefits from
Build reproducible, auditable infrastructure that can be version-controlled in git
objections: GraphQL API is too immature to build reliable automation on top of
If Terraform provider isn't maintained, IaC adoption is blocked
Vendor lock-in risk if export/migration paths aren't API-accessible
decision_triggers: Terraform provider exists and is actively maintained
REST API is stable with versioned endpoints and changelog
Webhook delivery has retry logic and failure logs
GitHub integration syncs bidirectionally without polling
sample_quotes: Your REST API is solid but the GraphQL endpoint has some rough edges. Plans to improve the schema?
If I can't provision it via Terraform, it's not going into our stack.
I need webhooks that actually retry on failure — fire-and-forget is not acceptable for fintech.
The goal is zero manual steps between a GitHub merge and a project status update.

journey_stages.mindset: Skeptical but thorough — will test API before committing
key_actions: Searches for Terraform provider on GitHub

journey_stages.mindset: Impatient with UI steps, wants CLI or API path for everything
Champion mode — will build internal tooling around the product if experience is good
key_actions: Advocates to engineering manager for broader rollout

source_evidence.claim: Automate team onboarding and project scaffolding via API
Set up event-driven webhook pipelines to sync updates
Integrate GitHub PRs and commits into project tracking
Build custom dashboards for engineering lead visibility
